reforms.description: France has introduced a 'Teacher’s Pact' aimed at coordinating and managing pedagogical innovation projects in schools, which is expected to enhance teaching quality and adapt the teaching culture.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving teaching practices and fostering innovation in education, the reform is likely to enhance student outcomes and contribute to a more skilled workforce.
reforms.status_evidence: The Teacher’s Pact was introduced for the start of the 2023 school year.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant effort to enhance teaching quality through innovation, which is crucial for improving educational outcomes.
reforms.source_quote: For the start of the 2023 school year, France has introduced a 'Teacher’s Pact' which includes missions to coordinate and manage one or more pedagogical innovation projects in schools.

reforms.description: France is building on its dynamic innovation ecosystems and strong scientific and technological skills to attract more R&D spending from multinationals, despite current R&D spending being below the OECD average.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Attracting R&D spending from multinationals can enhance local innovation capacity and productivity, contributing positively to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is implementing measures to attract R&D spending from multinationals.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform aims to enhance the existing innovation ecosystem and leverage France's strengths in science and technology, which could lead to significant improvements in R&D investment.
reforms.source_quote: Build on dynamic innovation ecosystems and a workforce with strong scientific and technological skills to attract R&D spending from multinationals.

reforms.description: France's government is considering lowering the ceiling of the R&D tax credit and increasing the subsidy rate to better support SMEs, which could enhance innovation and GDP growth.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By increasing the effectiveness of the R&D tax credit for SMEs, the reform aims to stimulate innovation and economic performance in the private sector.
reforms.status_evidence: The government is considering adjustments to the R&D tax credit to better support SMEs.
reforms.importance_rationale: The proposed adjustments to the R&D tax credit represent a significant change aimed at improving support for SMEs, which are crucial for innovation.
reforms.source_quote: Lowering the ceiling of the CIR and increasing the subsidy rate could allow for SMEs to benefit more and increase the effectiveness of the measure on innovation and GDP growth.

reforms.description: The innovation tax credit, which supports SME expenditure on designing prototypes and testing new products, totaled around 400 million euros in claims in 2021.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: This tax credit incentivizes SMEs to invest in product development, thereby fostering innovation and enhancing competitiveness.
reforms.status_evidence: The innovation tax credit totaled around 400 million euros in claims in 2021.
reforms.importance_rationale: The innovation tax credit is a significant measure aimed at supporting SMEs in their innovation efforts, contributing to overall economic growth.
reforms.source_quote: The innovation tax credit, which totalled around 400 million euros in claims in 2021, supports SME’s expenditure on designing prototypes and testing for new products.

reforms.description: The status of Young Innovative Companies (JEI) provides social and fiscal benefits for startups that meet specific R&D intensity and growth criteria.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By providing targeted benefits to innovative startups, this status encourages entrepreneurship and the development of new technologies.
reforms.status_evidence: The status of Young Innovative Companies (JEI) provides social and fiscal benefits for startups meeting specific R&D intensity and growth criteria.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative is significant for fostering innovation among startups, which are vital for economic dynamism and technological advancement.
reforms.source_quote: The status of Young Innovative Companies (Jeunes Entreprises Innovantes, JEI), which provides social and fiscal benefits for startups meeting specific R&D intensity and growth criteria.

reforms.description: France's public employment service has been restructured into France Travail, enhancing coordination among various stakeholders to improve employment support and training services.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: Improved coordination and support for employment services can enhance workforce skills and reduce unemployment, contributing to economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform officially took effect on 1 January 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant overhaul of the public employment service, aiming to improve employment outcomes and support for job seekers, which is crucial for economic recovery.
reforms.source_quote: Under the law on full employment (loi plein-emploi), Pôle Emploi became France Travail on 1 January 2024.

reforms.description: France's government has intensified its residential renovation programmes for 2024, aiming to encourage comprehensive renovations and increase the number of complete renovations to 200,000, a threefold increase from 2022.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving energy performance in residential buildings, the initiative supports job creation in the construction sector and enhances overall energy efficiency, contributing to long-term economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The government has increased expenditure ceilings in renovation programmes for 2024.
reforms.importance_rationale: The reform represents a significant increase in support for residential renovations, which is crucial for achieving energy efficiency targets and reducing emissions.
reforms.source_quote: France has several programmes to support and fund residential renovations, which were intensified for 2024, in part to encourage more comprehensive renovations.

reforms.description: The Renewable Energy Acceleration Act aims to streamline administrative procedures for renewable energy projects, enhancing local authority involvement and simplifying access to degraded land for development.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By accelerating the deployment of renewable energy projects, the Act is expected to enhance energy security and contribute to decarbonisation efforts, fostering economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Act was enacted in March 2023 to facilitate renewable energy project development.
reforms.importance_rationale: The Act represents a significant overhaul of the regulatory framework for renewable energy, aiming to boost investment and deployment in a critical sector for France's energy transition.
reforms.source_quote: The Renewable Energy Acceleration Act aims to remove all obstacles that delay the deployment of renewable energy projects.

reforms.description: The French government has introduced a career discovery programme in lower secondary schools, aimed at helping students understand the evolving labour market and their future career paths. This initiative will be implemented in stages, starting in the 2023 academic year and extending to all classes by 2024.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing career guidance, the programme aims to better align education with labour market needs, thereby improving employment outcomes for students.
reforms.status_evidence: The programme will be introduced into the second year of secondary school from the start of the 2023 academic year.
reforms.importance_rationale: This initiative represents a significant effort to improve career guidance in schools, which is crucial for adapting education to future labour market needs.
reforms.source_quote: The career discovery programme in lower secondary school will be introduced into the second year of secondary school from the start of the 2023 academic year.

reforms.description: The French government introduced a reform of vocational secondary schools in May 2023, allocating an additional one billion euros annually to enhance student pathways and strengthen ties with employers. This includes measures such as paid internships and the use of artificial intelligence to personalize student exercises.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By improving vocational training and aligning it with labor market needs, the reform aims to enhance the employability of graduates, thereby supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The reform measures are due to be implemented in the 2023, 2024, and 2025 academic years.
reforms.importance_rationale: This reform represents a significant investment in vocational education, aiming to address skill mismatches and improve alignment with labor market demands, which is crucial for economic development.
reforms.source_quote: The reform focuses on enhancing student pathways, strengthening ties with employers, and aligning training with changes in the labour market.

reforms.description: In 2023, the French Ministry of National Education and Youth unveiled its 2023-2027 digital strategy, aiming to ensure strong digital competences for all students and train 400,000 to 500,000 digital professionals.
reforms.growth_orientation_rationale: By enhancing digital skills among students, the strategy aims to prepare a workforce that can meet the demands of a rapidly evolving digital economy, thus supporting economic growth.
reforms.status_evidence: The Ministry unveiled its digital strategy aiming to train a significant number of digital professionals.
reforms.importance_rationale: This strategy represents a comprehensive approach to enhancing digital education and workforce readiness, which is critical for future economic competitiveness.
reforms.source_quote: The Ministry of National Education and Youth unveiled its 2023-2027 digital strategy, aiming to ensure strong digital competences for all students.
